title: Theory of Computation: Core Concepts & Hierarchy
description: A visual representation of the core computational models, their relationships, and the language hierarchy established by the Theory of Computation.
relationships: Turing Machines recognize Recursively Enumerable Languages
Regular Languages are a subset of Context-Free Languages
Context-Free Languages are a subset of Recursively Enumerable Languages
Turing Machines can solve Decidable Problems
The Halting Problem is a canonical example of an Undecidable Problem
